english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Colossians

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur, si consurrexistis cum Christo: quæ sursum sunt quærite, ubi Christus est in dextera Dei sedens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν συνηγέρθητε τῷ χριστῷ τὰ ἄνω ζητεῖτε οὗ ὁ χριστός ἐστιν ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ θεοῦ καθήμενος
verses.king_james: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ sursum sunt sapite, non quæ super terram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ ἄνω φρονεῖτε μὴ τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mortui enim estis, et vita vestra est abscondita cum Christo in Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεθάνετε γάρ καὶ ἡ ζωὴ ὑμῶν κέκρυπται σὺν τῷ χριστῷ ἐν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum Christus apparuerit, vita vestra: tunc et vos apparebitis cum ipso in gloria.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν ὁ χριστὸς φανερωθῇ ἡ ζωὴ ἡμῶν τότε καὶ ὑμεῖς σὺν αὐτῷ φανερωθήσεσθε ἐν δόξῃ
verses.king_james: When Christ, *who is* our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mortificate ergo membra vestra, quæ sunt super terram: fornicationem, immunditiam, libidinem, concupiscentiam malam, et avaritiam, quæ est simulacrorum servitus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νεκρώσατε οὖν τὰ μέλη ὑμῶν τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς πορνείαν ἀκαθαρσίαν πάθος ἐπιθυμίαν κακήν καὶ τὴν πλεονεξίαν ἥτις ἐστὶν εἰδωλολατρεία
verses.king_james: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:

verses.latin_vulgate: propter quæ venit ira Dei super filios incredulitatis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δι᾽ ἃ ἔρχεται ἡ ὀργὴ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ τοὺς υἱοὺς τῆς ἀπειθείας
verses.king_james: For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:

verses.latin_vulgate: in quibus et vos ambulastis aliquando, cum viveretis in illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν οἷς καὶ ὑμεῖς περιεπατήσατέ ποτε ὅτε ἐζῆτε ἐν αὔτοις
verses.king_james: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem deponite et vos omnia: iram, indignationem, malitiam, blasphemiam, turpem sermonem de ore vestro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ ἀπόθεσθε καὶ ὑμεῖς τὰ πάντα ὀργήν θυμόν κακίαν βλασφημίαν αἰσχρολογίαν ἐκ τοῦ στόματος ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite mentiri invicem, expoliantes vos veterem hominem cum actibus suis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ψεύδεσθε εἰς ἀλλήλους ἀπεκδυσάμενοι τὸν παλαιὸν ἄνθρωπον σὺν ταῖς πράξεσιν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;

verses.latin_vulgate: et induentes novum eum, qui renovatur in agnitionem secundum imaginem ejus qui creavit illum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐνδυσάμενοι τὸν νέον τὸν ἀνακαινούμενον εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν κατ᾽ εἰκόνα τοῦ κτίσαντος αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And have put on the new *man*, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi non est gentilis et Judæus, circumcisio et præputium, Barbarus et Scytha, servus et liber: sed omnia, et in omnibus Christus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπου οὐκ ἔνι ἕλλην καὶ ἰουδαῖος περιτομὴ καὶ ἀκροβυστία βάρβαρος σκύθης δοῦλος ἐλεύθερος ἀλλὰ τὰ πάντα καὶ ἐν πᾶσιν χριστός
verses.king_james: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond *nor* free: but Christ *is* all, and in all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Induite vos ergo, sicut electi Dei, sancti, et dilecti, viscera misericordiæ, benignitatem, humilitatem, modestiam, patientiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐνδύσασθε οὖν ὡς ἐκλεκτοὶ τοῦ θεοῦ ἅγιοι καὶ ἠγαπημένοι σπλάγχνα οἰκτιρμῶν χρηστότητα ταπεινοφροσύνην πρᾳότητα μακροθυμίαν
verses.king_james: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;

verses.latin_vulgate: supportantes invicem, et donantes vobismetipsis si quis adversus aliquem habet querelam: sicut et Dominus donavit vobis, ita et vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνεχόμενοι ἀλλήλων καὶ χαριζόμενοι ἑαυτοῖς ἐάν τις πρός τινα ἔχῃ μομφήν καθὼς καὶ ὁ χριστὸς ἐχαρίσατο ὑμῖν οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also *do* ye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super omnia autem hæc, caritatem habete, quod est vinculum perfectionis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπὶ πᾶσιν δὲ τούτοις τὴν ἀγάπην ἥτις ἐστιν σύνδεσμος τῆς τελειότητος
verses.king_james: And above all these things *put on* charity, which is the bond of perfectness.

verses.latin_vulgate: et pax Christi exsultet in cordibus vestris, in qua et vocati estis in uno corpore: et grati estote.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἡ εἰρήνη τοῦ θεοῦ βραβευέτω ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν εἰς ἣν καὶ ἐκλήθητε ἐν ἑνὶ σώματι καὶ εὐχάριστοι γίνεσθε
verses.king_james: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbum Christi habitet in vobis abundanter, in omni sapientia, docentes, et commonentes vosmetipsos, psalmis, hymnis, et canticis spiritualibus, in gratia cantantes in cordibus vestris Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λόγος τοῦ χριστοῦ ἐνοικείτω ἐν ὑμῖν πλουσίως ἐν πάσῃ σοφίᾳ διδάσκοντες καὶ νουθετοῦντες ἑαυτοὺς ψαλμοῖς καὶ ὕμνοις καὶ ᾠδαῖς πνευματικαῖς ἐν χάριτι ᾄδοντες ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ ὑμῶν τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omne, quodcumque facitis in verbo aut in opere, omnia in nomine Domini Jesu Christi, gratias agentes Deo et Patri per ipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶν ὅ τι ἂν ποιῆτε ἐν λόγῳ ἢ ἐν ἔργῳ πάντα ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου ἰησοῦ εὐχαριστοῦντες τῷ θεῷ καὶ πατρὶ δι᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, *do* all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulieres, subditæ estote viris, sicut oportet, in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱ γυναῖκες ὑποτάσσεσθε τοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνδράσιν ὡς ἀνῆκεν ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viri, diligite uxores vestras, et nolite amari esse ad illas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ ἄνδρες ἀγαπᾶτε τὰς γυναῖκας καὶ μὴ πικραίνεσθε πρὸς αὐτάς
verses.king_james: Husbands, love *your* wives, and be not bitter against them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii, obedite parentibus per omnia: hoc enim placitum est in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ τέκνα ὑπακούετε τοῖς γονεῦσιν κατὰ πάντα τοῦτο γὰρ ἐστιν εὐάρεστόν τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Children, obey *your* parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Patres, nolite ad indignationem provocare filios vestros, ut non pusillo animo fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ πατέρες μὴ ἐρεθίζετε τὰ τέκνα ὑμῶν ἵνα μὴ ἀθυμῶσιν
verses.king_james: Fathers, provoke not your children *to anger*, lest they be discouraged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servi, obedite per omnia dominis carnalibus, non ad oculum servientes, quasi hominibus placentes, sed in simplicitate cordis, timentes Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δοῦλοι ὑπακούετε κατὰ πάντα τοῖς κατὰ σάρκα κυρίοις μὴ ἐν ὀφθαλμοδουλείαις ὡς ἀνθρωπάρεσκοι ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἁπλότητι καρδίας φοβούμενοι τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Servants, obey in all things *your* masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quodcumque facitis, ex animo operamini sicut Domino, et non hominibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶν ὃ τι ἐὰν ποιῆτε ἐκ ψυχῆς ἐργάζεσθε ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: And whatsoever ye do, do *it* heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;

verses.latin_vulgate: scientes quod a Domino accipietis retributionem hæreditatis. Domino Christo servite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδότες ὅτι ἀπὸ κυρίου ἀπολήψεσθε τὴν ἀνταπόδοσιν τῆς κληρονομίας τῷ γὰρ κυρίῳ χριστῷ δουλεύετε
verses.king_james: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim injuriam facit, recipiet id quod inique gessit: et non est personarum acceptio apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀδικῶν κομιεῖται ὃ ἠδίκησεν καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν προσωποληψία
verses.king_james: But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.
